summary: Persistent SYSTEM-level service with self-updating remote command channel and host telemetry exfiltration
details:
On `npm install` with administrator privileges, scripts/post-install.js silently registers a Windows service (`claw-subagent-service`) running as LocalSystem with auto-start and infinite auto-restart (sc.exe failure... reset=0 actions=restart/0/restart/0/restart/0), pointing at service/daemon.js. The daemon establishes three concurrent installer-harm mechanisms:

1. Self-updating channel: service/updater.js runs `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@<latest>` every 6 hours (setInterval CHECK_INTERVAL) with no signature, hash, or version-pin verification. Any future version published to this npm name is auto-deployed to every installed host with SYSTEM/root privileges within hours.

2. Remote command channel: service/worker.js connects to a RongCloud IM tenant (App Key `bmdehs6pbyyks`, token endpoint `https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im/api/claw/token/<nodeId>`). Inbound IM messages are dispatched by service/modules/rongyun-message-handler.js to `executeCommand`, which runs start/stop/restart/CONFIG_FIX scripts via cmd/bash, forwards input to an `openclaw agent` subprocess, and on `device_control:delete`/`disable` uninstalls the service. The author-controlled IM tenant is a live remote-command surface against installer hosts.

3. Periodic host telemetry exfiltration: service/modules/heartbeat-dashboard.js sends chunks every 30 seconds containing MAC address, OS platform/arch/cpu/memory/uptime, AI session contexts (model, provider, token counts) from `~/.openclaw/agents/*/sessions/*.jsonl`, project list, task list, and usage events with timestamps and costs, all routed to the same author IM tenant. Heartbeat every 20s additionally sends MAC + nodeName.

Additionally, when running as SYSTEM, worker.js enumerates `C:\Users` looking for any user's `.claw-bridge/config.json` or `.openclaw/` directory and adopts the first match as its effective home, allowing the SYSTEM-context daemon to pick up another user's bridge credentials.

The combination — silent install-time SYSTEM persistence + unverified self-update + author-controlled remote command channel + cross-user credential adoption + continuous host fingerprint and agent-activity exfiltration — places the installer's machine under sustained author control regardless of the documented "remote management" purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
